batch_5_search_plan.hard_deadline: Tuesday 2026-05-19 (Day 7). Archive leads immediately if missed. No deferrals.
batch_5_search_plan.7_day_rule: Non-negotiable after 44-session warm pipeline failure. HANDS executes Day-3 or Day-6 — never Day-44.

sector_priority.note: One or two likely in Malton — prioritise first. Post-reference note 3-4x higher acceptance.
Helmsley fine dining owners often post on LinkedIn
Search only if no higher-priority leads in that town

Malton.sector_note: Solicitors and estate agents first — highest LinkedIn presence in Malton market town

Pickering.sector_note: B&B and tourism leads first — higher LinkedIn presence than trades

Helmsley.sector_note: Affluent market — highest post-reference likelihood in the Ryedale batch. Check posts for last 60 days.

Kirkbymoorside.sector_note: Try both spellings — Kirkbymoorside / Kirbymoorside is a common variant in profiles

Hovingham.sector_note: Small village, low LinkedIn presence. Use generic note if profile found — post-reference unlikely.

Norton-on-Derwent.sector_note: Generic note for most trades. Professional services: use fallback_1 with sector term.

ssl_leads.note: Do NOT mention SSL, HTTPS, or 'Not Secure' in the LinkedIn note. Treat as cold connection — no email reference.
ssl_leads.query_note: Standard primary query. Profile confirmation matters — SSL leads often have minimal social presence.

mobile_leads.note: Standard query. Mobile-signal leads often skew slightly younger — marginally higher LinkedIn likelihood.
mobile_leads.fallback_note: If primary query fails: try first name + town + sector separately without business name in quotes.

no_website_leads.note: Owner name may not be known — sourced from Google Maps listing only.
no_website_leads.query_note: If only business name known (no owner surname): site:linkedin.com/in [sector] "[business name]" OR site:linkedin.com/in [business name] "[town]"
no_website_leads.skip_rule: If first name only (no surname confirmed): mark profile_confirmed_owner: false. Do not send note unless profile unmistakably matches the business.

aida_leads.note: High-review leads — scrutinise profile carefully before sending. These leads have strong reputations and are more selective.
aida_leads.query_note: Standard primary query. Verify the profile matches before sending.

entry_template_for_hands.instruction: For each lead sent this morning (Batch 5), add one entry to search_queries[] at the top of this file using this structure:

schema.name: [owner_full_name or UNKNOWN_IF_NOT_AVAILABLE]
schema.linkedin_priority: [HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW — based on sector priority table above]
